variants.name: Blocksignal
variants.blurb: Single block signal, Signal to use in blocks, without distant signal head

aspects.name: Hp0 (Halt)
aspects.whatItMeans: Stop. You must not pass this signal at danger without authority. The line ahead may be occupied, set against you, or unsafe.
aspects.whatYouDo: Stop at the signal (at the stop line, before the signal). Wait for it to clear, or for instructions.

aspects.name: Hp1 (Fahrt)
aspects.whatItMeans: The line ahead is clear for at least the next three signals. You may run at the maximum permitted line speed.
aspects.whatYouDo: Proceed normally at line speed. Keep looking ahead — the next signal could be showing anything.

variants.name: Single distant signal
variants.blurb: Single distant signal ahead of block signals. It has an Ne2 plate.

aspects.name: Vr0 (Halt erwarten)
aspects.whatItMeans: Expect stop, prepare to stop at next main signal

aspects.name: Vr1 (Fahrt erwarten)
aspects.whatItMeans: Expect proceed, prepare to proceed at next main signal

aspects.name: Vr2 (Langsamfahrt erwarten)
aspects.whatItMeans: Expect slow, prepare to proceed slowly at next main signal

aspects.name: Vr0 (Halt erwarten) Short
aspects.whatItMeans: Expect stop, prepare to stop at next main signal. Placed instead of the "normal" single distant signal when the distance to the block signal is less than 1km. It has a white light (kennlicht) on the left side of the "usual" distant signal head.
aspects.lookAlikes: This is the same as a distant signal repeater which looks identical except without the Ne 2 board on the pole.

aspects.name: Vr1 (Fahrt erwarten) Short
aspects.whatItMeans: Expect proceed, prepare to proceed at next main signal. Placed instead of the "normal" single distant signal when the distance to the block signal is less than 1km. It has a white light (kennlicht) on the left side of the "usual" distant signal head.
aspects.lookAlikes: This is the same as a distant signal repeater which looks identical except without the Ne 2 board on the pole.

aspects.name: Vr2 (Langsamfahrt erwarten) Short
aspects.whatItMeans: Expect slow, prepare to proceed slowly at next main signal. Placed instead of the "normal" single distant signal when the distance to the block signal is less than 1km. It has a white light (kennlicht) on the left side of the "usual" distant signal head.
aspects.lookAlikes: This is the same as a distant signal repeater which looks identical except without the Ne 2 board on the pole.

variants.name: Combined Block + Distant
variants.blurb: Signal to use in blocks, with distant signal head. If main signal shows stop (Hp0) then distant signal is dark.

aspects.name: Hp0 (Halt)
aspects.whatItMeans: Previously one red light had the meaning of "Stop for train movements" (shunting movements were allowed). To actually stop shunting two horizontal red lights were neccessary. Since 1959 one red light is enough to stop all movements.

aspects.name: Hp1 + Vr0 (Fahrt + Halt erwarten)
aspects.meaning: Proceed preparing to stop at next main signal
aspects.whatItMeans: Proceed preparing to stop at next main signal
aspects.whatYouDo: Continue being ready to stop at the next signal.

aspects.name: Hp1 + Vr1 (Fahrt + Fahrt erwarten)
aspects.whatItMeans: Proceed preparing to proceed at next main signal

aspects.name: Hp1 + Vr2 (Fahrt + Langsamfahrt erwarten)
aspects.meaning: Proceed preparing to proceed slowly at next main signal
aspects.whatItMeans: Proceed preparing to proceed slowly at next main signal
aspects.whatYouDo: Proceed preparing to proceed slowly at next main signal
